entries.title: American Consumers Pressured as Rising Prices Erase Tax Refunds
entries.summary: U.S. consumers are reportedly facing increased financial pressure as tax refunds peter out. Economists and executives are warning that this cash crunch comes as fuel costs related to the Iran war impacts the economy, the Financial Times (FT) reported Monday (May 25). entries.title: Robinhood’s Purchase of WonderFi OK’d by Canadian Regulators
entries.summary: Canadian regulators have approved Robinhood’s bid to acquire digital asset products and services company WonderFi. entries.title: Bank Loans to UK Businesses Hit 28-Year Low
entries.summary: Lending by banks to businesses in the U.K. has reportedly reached a 28-year low. That’s according to a report Monday (May 25) from the Financial Times (FT), which noted that this trend is being driven by weak economic growth and stricter regulation on lenders, making credit harder to access for smaller businesses in particular. entries.title: Branch International Cuts Staff in Africa Amid $30 Million Profit
entries.summary: Visa-backed digital lending startup Branch International is reportedly cutting staff at its Kenyan and Nigerian offices. entries.title: Italian state lender to raise stake in Nexi
entries.summary: Italy’s state lender Cassa Depositi e Presti is set to increase its stake Nexi, strengthening its hold on the strategically important payments giant, which has attracted takeover interest from US private equity group CVC.

entries.title: SmartComply bids to help UK firms reopen African payment corridors
entries.summary: Lagos-based compliance and cybersecurity company SmartComply has launched in the UK, making its AI-powered anti-money laundering platform available to British payment firms serving African markets.
